annotations.label: Simulated annealing

anchor.snippet: is a probabilistic technique for approximating the global optimum

annotations.note: Simulated annealing as a metaheuristic optimization algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib, which contains no annealing or Metropolis-type search procedures.

annotations.label: Metropolis–Hastings algorithm

anchor.snippet: The method is an adaptation of the Metropolis–Hastings algorithm

annotations.note: The Metropolis–Hastings MCMC sampler is not formalized in Mathlib; Mathlib has no Markov-chain Monte Carlo machinery.

annotations.label: Energy function analogy

anchor.snippet: is analogous to the internal energy of the system in that state

annotations.note: The physical energy-function analogy used to motivate simulated annealing has no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Basic iteration step

anchor.snippet: probabilistically decides between moving the system to state

annotations.note: The probabilistic accept/reject iteration of simulated annealing is an algorithm not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Move

anchor.snippet: is called a move

annotations.note: The notion of a 'move' between neighboring states in a search space is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: TSP neighbors as permutations

anchor.snippet: each state is typically defined as a permutation of the cities to be visited

annotations.note: Permutations are formalized as `Equiv.Perm` (confirmed), but the TSP state space and its neighbor structure are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hill climbing gets stuck in local optima

anchor.snippet: is not guaranteed to lead to any of the existing better solutions

annotations.note: The claim that greedy hill climbing can get trapped in local optima is an algorithmic observation absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Acceptance probability function

anchor.snippet: is specified by an acceptance probability function

annotations.note: The acceptance probability function P(e, e', T) of simulated annealing is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Acceptance probability must stay positive

anchor.snippet: must be positive even when

annotations.note: This requirement on the acceptance function is specific to simulated annealing and not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Limit behavior as temperature tends to zero

anchor.snippet: and to a positive value otherwise

annotations.note: The T→0 limiting behavior of the acceptance probability is a statement about the SA function, which Mathlib does not contain.

annotations.label: Reduction to greedy algorithm at T=0

anchor.snippet: the procedure reduces to the greedy algorithm

annotations.note: The reduction of simulated annealing to a greedy descent in the zero-temperature limit is an algorithmic statement absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Original always-downhill rule

anchor.snippet: the procedure always moved downhill when it found a way to do so

annotations.note: The original Metropolis always-downhill acceptance rule is an algorithmic convention with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Acceptance decreases with uphill size

anchor.snippet: small uphill moves are more likely than large ones

annotations.note: This monotonicity property of the SA acceptance probability is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Role of temperature in sensitivity

anchor.snippet: plays a crucial role in controlling the evolution

annotations.note: The role of the temperature parameter in SA dynamics is not a Mathlib concept.

annotations.label: Annealing schedule

anchor.snippet: it is decreased at each step following some annealing schedule

annotations.note: The temperature annealing schedule is specific to simulated annealing and not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convergence to global optimum

anchor.snippet: the probability that the simulated annealing algorithm terminates with a global optimal solution approaches 1

annotations.note: The asymptotic convergence theorem for simulated annealing is not formalized; Mathlib has no SA Markov chain convergence result.

annotations.label: Required parameters

anchor.snippet: one must specify the following parameters

annotations.note: The list of parameters one must supply to run simulated annealing is implementation guidance not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Random walk on search graph

anchor.snippet: may be modeled as a random walk on a search graph

annotations.note: Grep found no random-walk formalization in Mathlib, and the SA search-graph model is absent.

annotations.label: Small diameter requirement

anchor.snippet: it must provide a sufficiently short path on this graph

annotations.note: This heuristic requirement on the search graph's diameter for SA is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: TSP state count for n=20

anchor.snippet: the search space for n = 20 cities has n! = 2,432,902,008,176,640,000 (2.4 quintillion) states

annotations.note: The factorial function `Nat.factorial` is formalized (confirmed) so 20! is computable, but the TSP state-counting example itself is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transition probability

anchor.snippet: the transition probability is defined as the probability that the simulated annealing algorithm will move to state

annotations.note: The SA-specific transition probability (generator times acceptance) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Specification is partially redundant

anchor.snippet: is partially redundant

annotations.note: This observation about redundancy in specifying SA's generator and acceptance functions is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kirkpatrick acceptance function

anchor.snippet: In the formulation of the method by Kirkpatrick et al.

annotations.note: The Kirkpatrick exponential acceptance function is specific to simulated annealing and not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deterministic update speeds optimization

anchor.snippet: proposed that a deterministic update

annotations.note: The proposal that a deterministic update accelerates SA is an algorithmic claim absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deterministic update is optimal

anchor.snippet: the deterministic update strategy is indeed the optimal one

annotations.note: The optimality theorem for the deterministic SA update strategy is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Skew generator toward similar energy

anchor.snippet: one should skew the generator towards candidate moves

annotations.note: This candidate-generation heuristic for SA is not a Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: TSP consecutive vs arbitrary swaps

anchor.snippet: swapping two consecutive cities in a low-energy tour is expected to have a modest effect on its energy

annotations.note: This TSP example about swap moves and energy change is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Precise candidate heuristic

anchor.snippet: A more precise statement of the heuristic is that one should try the first candidate states

annotations.note: This refined SA candidate-ordering heuristic is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deep local minima

anchor.snippet: states (or sets of connected states) that have much lower energy than all its neighboring states

annotations.note: The notion of deep local minima in an SA energy landscape is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catchment basins trap the algorithm

anchor.snippet: may trap the simulated annealing algorithm with high probability

annotations.note: The claim that catchment basins trap SA with high probability is an algorithmic statement absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: No generator meets both goals

anchor.snippet: it is impossible to design a candidate generator that will satisfy this goal

annotations.note: This impossibility observation about SA candidate generators is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: TSP two-tour deep basins

anchor.snippet: it is not hard to exhibit two tours

annotations.note: This TSP example exhibiting two deep-basin tours is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Near-equilibrium cooling assumption

anchor.snippet: assumes that the cooling rate is low enough

annotations.note: The quasi-equilibrium slow-cooling assumption of SA is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relaxation time

anchor.snippet: the time one must wait for the equilibrium to be restored after a change in temperature

annotations.note: The relaxation time of an SA Markov chain is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Restarting

anchor.snippet: This process is called restarting of simulated annealing

annotations.note: The restart strategy for simulated annealing is an algorithmic technique not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interacting Metropolis–Hastings

anchor.snippet: combines simulated annealing moves with an acceptance-rejection of the best-fitted individuals

annotations.note: Interacting Metropolis–Hastings / particle-filter SA is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quantum annealing

anchor.snippet: uses "quantum fluctuations" instead of thermal fluctuations

annotations.note: Quantum annealing is an optimization metaheuristic not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic tunneling

anchor.snippet: attempts to overcome the increasing difficulty simulated annealing runs have in escaping from local minima

annotations.note: Stochastic tunneling is an optimization method with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Tabu search

anchor.snippet: normally moves to neighbouring states of lower energy

annotations.note: Tabu search is a metaheuristic not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual-phase evolution

anchor.snippet: is a family of algorithms and processes

annotations.note: Dual-phase evolution is an algorithmic family not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reactive search optimization

anchor.snippet: focuses on combining machine learning with optimization

annotations.note: Reactive search optimization is a metaheuristic not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genetic algorithms

anchor.snippet: maintain a pool of solutions rather than just one

annotations.note: Genetic algorithms are an optimization metaheuristic with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Memetic algorithms

anchor.snippet: search for solutions by employing a set of agents that both cooperate and compete

annotations.note: Memetic algorithms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graduated optimization

anchor.snippet: digressively "smooths" the target function while optimizing

annotations.note: Graduated optimization (continuation) as an algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ant colony optimization

anchor.snippet: uses many ants (or agents) to traverse the solution space

annotations.note: Ant colony optimization is a swarm metaheuristic not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cross-entropy method

anchor.snippet: generates candidate solutions via a parameterized probability distribution

annotations.note: The cross-entropy optimization method is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmony search

anchor.snippet: mimics musicians in improvisation

annotations.note: Harmony search is a metaheuristic with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Stochastic optimization

anchor.snippet: is an umbrella set of methods that includes simulated annealing

annotations.note: Stochastic optimization as an algorithmic umbrella is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Particle swarm optimization

anchor.snippet: is an algorithm modeled on swarm intelligence

annotations.note: Particle swarm optimization is a swarm metaheuristic not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Runner-root algorithm

anchor.snippet: is a meta-heuristic optimization algorithm for solving unimodal and multimodal problems

annotations.note: The runner-root algorithm is a metaheuristic not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Intelligent water drops

anchor.snippet: mimics the behavior of natural water drops

annotations.note: The intelligent water drops algorithm is a metaheuristic with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Parallel tempering

anchor.snippet: is a simulation of model copies at different temperatures

annotations.note: Parallel tempering (replica exchange MCMC) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multi-objective simulated annealing

anchor.snippet: have been used in multi-objective optimization

annotations.note: Multi-objective simulated annealing is an algorithmic variant absent from Mathlib.
